_comment: Statusline config. Edit templates, weather location and colors here without touching statusline.py. This file MUST live in the same folder as statusline.py. Colors live in the 'colors' block below (hex #RRGGBB), referenced from templates as {c.NAME}; any color key omitted there keeps the COLORS default in statusline.py. If this file is missing/invalid, statusline.py falls back to its built-in defaults. Keys starting with _ are ignored.
_comment_emoji_width: How many terminal columns one emoji occupies (1 or 2). Used only to align the line2 context bar under the line1 segment. Most modern terminals (Windows Terminal, iTerm2) render emoji 2-wide; set 1 if your terminal renders them single-width and the bar looks shifted. Anything other than 1 means 2.
_comment_ctx_bar_empty: Glyph used for the context bar's UNFILLED cells (the ░ track). Any single-column character works, e.g. ░ ▒ ▓ · - or a single space. Colored with the ctx_bar_track color. Should be 1 column wide to keep the bar alignment correct.

weather._comment: City name + coordinates for the Open-Meteo API (fetched only when an active line uses {weather} or {sun}). show_* booleans pick which parts of the {weather} string appear (all default true). The editor's 'Look up coordinates' action geocodes the name for you.
weather.name: Newyork

status._comment: Claude status-page incident indicator. Renders the SINGLE most-recent ACTIVE incident from status.claude.com's RSS feed via {status} ({status:N} caps the label+title at N chars, 0 = unlimited, like {ctx_bar:N}; {status_icon} = emoji only). Only incidents updated within max_age_hours (default 48; 0 = no limit) count, so stale incidents drop off. {status_header} is a bold, status_header-colored banner showing the `title` text (put any emoji/ascii right in the title; a leading emoji is auto-spaced from the words) shown ONLY when something is active - put it on its own line ABOVE {status} for a 2-line block that appears/disappears together (show_header=false suppresses it). Cached in ~/.claude/status_cache.json (conditional GET); the network is touched only when a line uses one of these placeholders. show_* pick which pieces of the incident line appear; '(+N)' hints at other active incidents. Everything is empty (its line hidden) when nothing is active. include_maintenance also surfaces scheduled / in-progress maintenance.
status.title: ⚠️ Claude have some issues

templates._comment: Available placeholders: {c.COLORNAME} apply color, {r} reset, {time} {version} {model} {effort} {ctx} {ctx_percent} {ctx_bar} {align_pad} {total} {input} {output} {cached} {path} {branch} {added} {removed} {weather} {sun} {peak_label} {status} {status:N} {status_icon} {status_header}. {status} shows the latest active incident within max_age_hours (capped at status.max_len chars); {status:N} caps it at N chars for this line (0 = unlimited), like {ctx_bar:N}. {status_header} is the bold banner; both are empty (line hidden) when nothing is active. Bare {ctx_bar} auto-sizes/positions to align line2's gauge with the line1 segment from {effort} to end-of-line; put {align_pad} right before it for the leading spaces. Use {ctx_bar:N} for a FIXED N-cell bar (e.g. {ctx_bar:30}, no {align_pad} needed). Eighth-block precision either way. Lines are printed in order; remove a line to hide it. A line that renders empty (e.g. {status} when nothing is broken) is skipped entirely.

colors._comment: Color overrides (hex #RRGGBB), used in templates as {c.KEY}. Delete a key to fall back to the statusline.py default. Invalid values are ignored. ctx_bar = filled gauge blocks, ctx_bar_track = empty ░ cells, ctx_bracket = the ▐ ▌ edges.
